pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic test data created via SQL INSERT statements in setup_task.sh
pi_items.source: Analysis of setup_task.sh script: drugs are created with INSERT INTO drugs(...) statements in LibreHealth EHR Docker container. Initial on_hand values: Ibuprofen 200mg=500 units, Metformin 500mg=300 units. Initial reorder_point values: both=0.

privileged_info_summary: Ibuprofen 200mg and Metformin 500mg are real, FDA-approved medications. Ibuprofen is an OTC pain reliever/fever reducer, and Metformin is a first-line type 2 diabetes treatment. The task involves setting reorder points (inventory thresholds) to 150 and 60 respectively via the LibreHealth EHR UI. These reorder point values are arbitrary test parameters set by the task creator, not derived from real pharmacy data, clinical guidelines, or standard inventory management calculations. All data is synthetic: the drugs are created in a Docker-based LibreHealth EHR test environment with test inventory quantities (500 units Ibuprofen, 300 units Metformin).
